editableNewDialogue.text: Can we switch back to normal replies (not emojis/roleplay) and just chat about games for a sec?
Sure. What kind of game discussion—recommendations, history/trivia, or helping decide what to play next?
Give me one interesting trivia fact about EarthBound’s development, and one about a totally unrelated game.

EarthBound: Shigesato Itoi (a copywriter/essayist, not a traditional game developer) led the project and pushed an offbeat, contemporary-Japan-meets-surreal-Americana tone that made it stand out from typical RPGs of the era.
Unrelated game (Tetris): The original versions spread widely through informal copying before modern licensing was settled, which helped it explode in popularity across different platforms.
Now a hypothetical: if someone is making a retro RPG today, what’s one design choice that helps it feel modern?

Add friction-reducers without changing the core vibe—e.g., fast battle animations plus optional auto-battle, clear quest log/map markers (toggleable), and generous save/quick-save so experimentation doesn’t waste time.
Been replaying MOTHER 2 on the bus lately—those rides feel shorter when I'm grinding for cash in Winters.
Nice, that stretch can eat time in the best way. Winters has such a specific vibe.
You remember when I said I like MOTHER (Earthbound Zero/Beginnings)?
Correction: I actually *don’t* like MOTHER (Earthbound Zero/Beginnings). I mixed it up with MOTHER 2 (EarthBound), which is the one I like.
Got it—you don’t like MOTHER (Beginnings); you like MOTHER 2 (EarthBound).

turns.text: Correction: I actually *don’t* like MOTHER (Earthbound Zero/Beginnings). I mixed it up with MOTHER 2 (EarthBound), which is the one I like.
